title: Departure From Life.
content:
Two are the roads that before thee lie open from life to conduct thee;
    To the ideal one leads thee, the other to death.
    See that while yet thou art free, on the first thou commencest thy journey,
    Ere by the merciless fates on to the other thou'rt led!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Friedrich Schiller, titled "Departure From Life.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
